---
title: "Module_01_WKSP_02"
author: "Justin Proctor"
date: "2026-05-20"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## MB5370 Module 1: Programming Fundamentals

## Workshop 1: Introduction/Foundations
```{r}
source("Module_01_WKSP_01.R")
```
## Workshop 2: Visualization using ggplot2

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r first plot with mpg dataset}
library(tidyverse)
ggplot(data = mpg) + 
  geom_point(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy))
```


```{r point color by class using the "mpg" dataset}
ggplot(data = mpg) + 
  geom_point(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy, colour = class))
```

```{r point size by class}
ggplot(data = mpg) + 
  geom_point(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy, size = class))
# Warning: Using size for a discrete variable is not advised. 
```

```{r color transparency by class}
ggplot(data = mpg) + 
  geom_point(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy, alpha = class))
```

```{r point shape by class}
ggplot(data = mpg) + 
  geom_point(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy, shape = class))
```

```{r blue points}
ggplot(data = mpg) + 
  geom_point(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy), color = "blue")
```

```{r when color equals display less than 5}
ggplot(data = mpg) + 
  geom_point(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy, color = displ < 5))
```

```{r using facet wrap}
ggplot(data = mpg) + 
  geom_point(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy)) + 
  facet_wrap(~ class, nrow = 2)
```

```{r using facet grid}
ggplot(data = mpg) + 
  geom_point(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy)) + 
  facet_grid(drv ~ cyl)
```

```{r replacing "drv" with "."}
ggplot(data = mpg) + 
  geom_point(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy)) + 
  facet_grid(. ~ cyl)
```

```{r display data as points}
ggplot(data = mpg) + 
  geom_point(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy))
```

```{r display data as smooth line}
ggplot(data = mpg) + 
  geom_smooth(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy))
```

```{r}
ggplot(data = mpg) + 
#  geom_point(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy)) # points horrible 
  geom_smooth(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy)) # try smooth line
```

```{r separating cars into their drive type}
ggplot(data = mpg) + 
  geom_smooth(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy, linetype = drv, color = drv))
```

```{r setting group aesthetic}
 ggplot(data = mpg) +
  geom_smooth(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy, group = drv))
```

```{r changing color}
ggplot(data = mpg) +
  geom_smooth(
    mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy, color = drv),
    show.legend = FALSE,
  )
```

```{r multiple geoms}
ggplot(data = mpg) + 
  geom_point(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy)) +
  geom_smooth(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy))
```

```{r same as above with programmatic efficiency}
ggplot(data = mpg, mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy)) + 
  geom_point() + 
  geom_smooth()
```

```{r different aesthetics in different layers}
ggplot(data = mpg, mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy)) + 
  geom_point(mapping = aes(color = class)) + 
  geom_smooth()
```

```{r filtering to plot only a subset of the data}
ggplot(data = mpg, mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy)) + 
  geom_point(mapping = aes(color = class)) + 
  geom_smooth(data = filter(mpg, class == "subcompact"), se = FALSE)
```

```{r coding exercise}
ggplot(data = mpg, mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy)) + 
  geom_point() + 
  geom_smooth()

ggplot() + 
  geom_point(data = mpg, mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy)) + 
  geom_smooth(data = mpg, mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy))

#graphs look the same because conditons set are the same, just established at different points along the code
```

```{r bar chart utilizing "diamonds" dataset}
ggplot(data = diamonds) + 
  geom_bar(mapping = aes(x = cut))
```

```{r recreating above using stat count}
ggplot(data = diamonds) + 
  stat_count(mapping = aes(x = cut))
```

```{r overrididing a default stat using tribble function}
demo <- tribble(
  ~cut,         ~freq,
  "Fair",       1610,
  "Good",       4906,
  "Very Good",  12082,
  "Premium",    13791,
  "Ideal",      21551
)
demo

ggplot(data = demo) +
  geom_bar(mapping = aes(x = cut, y = freq), stat = "identity")
```

```{r displaying a bar chart of the proportion of total diamond dataset rather than count}
ggplot(data = diamonds) + 
  geom_bar(mapping = aes(x = cut, y = stat(prop), group = 1))
# Warning: `stat(prop)` was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0.
```

```{r showing more transparency on transformation details}
ggplot(data = diamonds) + 
  stat_summary(
    mapping = aes(x = cut, y = depth),
    fun.min = min,
    fun.max = max,
    fun = median
  )
```

```{r changing aesthetics of bar chart}
ggplot(data = diamonds) + 
  geom_bar(mapping = aes(x = cut, colour = cut))
ggplot(data = diamonds) + 
  geom_bar(mapping = aes(x = cut, fill = cut))
```

```{r changing color by clarity variable}
ggplot(data = diamonds) + 
  geom_bar(mapping = aes(x = cut, fill = clarity))
```

```{r position adjustments to customize plots by identity}
#To alter transparency (alpha)
ggplot(data = diamonds, mapping = aes(x = cut, fill = clarity)) + 
  geom_bar(alpha = 1/5, position = "identity")

#To color the bar outlines with no fill color
ggplot(data = diamonds, mapping = aes(x = cut, colour = clarity)) + 
  geom_bar(fill = NA, position = "identity")
```

```{r position adjustments to customize plots by fill}
ggplot(data = diamonds) + 
  geom_bar(mapping = aes(x = cut, fill = clarity), position = "fill")
```

```{r position adjustments to customize plots by dodge}
ggplot(data = diamonds) + 
  geom_bar(mapping = aes(x = cut, fill = clarity), position = "dodge")
```

```{r position adjustments to customize plots by jitter}
ggplot(data = mpg) + 
  geom_point(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy), position = "jitter")
```



<!-- TEMPLATE FOR USING GGPLOT: -->

<!-- ggplot(data = <DATA>) + -->
<!--   <GEOM_FUNCTION>( -->
<!--      mapping = aes(<MAPPINGS>), -->
<!--      stat = <STAT>, -->
<!--      position = <POSITION> -->
<!--   ) + -->
<!--   <FACET_FUNCTION> -->



```{r}
view(penguins)
```

```{r penguin count by species and island}
ggplot(data = penguins) + 
  geom_bar(mapping = aes(x = island, fill = species))
```

```{r penguin count by species and island with dodge positioning}
ggplot(data = penguins) + 
  geom_bar(mapping = aes(x = island, fill = species), position = "dodge")
```
